algorithm: This new algorithm enhances the decision-making process by dynamically adjusting the weights of distance and penalty based on how many customers are left to visit, placing a higher emphasis on penalty reduction towards the end of the route to optimize time windows compliance.

The new algorithm employs a nearest neighbor heuristic penalized by time-window violations, and enhanced by a collaborative avoidance scoring that penalizes selections of customers that are closer to other trucks than the current truck, fostering dispersion rather than concentration of trucks.

The algorithm employs a dynamic priority scheduling strategy, factoring in urgency based on proximity to customer time windows and considering the waiting strategy dynamically based on other trucks’ distances and windows alignment, hence optimizing travel and wait times.

The new algorithm employs a dynamic proximity approach which intensifies collaborative penalties based on the time proximity to customer availability, effectively modifying collaborative avoidance in relation to how urgent a customer needs service.

This algorithm utilizes a dynamic adaptive weighting system for penalties: customer urgency is weighed more heavily as the time window narrows, and collaborative dispersion weights adapt based on geographic cluster densities of trucks versus customers.

This algorithm uses a blending approach that considers the relative distance to the node, alignment with the time window, and proximity to other trucks as factors in a weighted score system to determine the next node to visit.

algorithm considers both immediate profit and future path optimization by estimating both direct costs to each available customer and implied costs considering subsequent potential moves, adjusting dynamically with a congestion-aware approach

The new algorithm introduces a dynamic priority system that accounts for urgency of customer service based on the proximity to the closing of their time window, and combines this with an adaptive weighting of routes based on current truck congestion at potential next nodes, maximizing both individual and fleet efficiency.

The new algorithm applies a dynamic heuristic approach that balances the nearest neighbor strategy with time window compliance, and has an adaptive collaboration score that dynamically reduces the influence of nearby trucks based on distance and relative speed to encourage strategic dispersion and timely customer service.
